items.title: CMS guidance: limits on federal Medicaid/CHIP matching funds for certain noncitizens start Oct. 1, 2026
items.summary: CMS issued a State Health Official letter to prepare states for an Oct. 1, 2026 change that limits the ability to claim federal matching funds for full Medicaid and CHIP benefits for people who are not U.S. citizens/nationals or who don’t fall into specified noncitizen categories. CMS notes exceptions including continued federal matching for emergency Medicaid and that the change does not apply to states’ option to cover lawfully residing children and pregnant women in Medicaid/CHIP.

items.title: HUD rescinds PIH 2024-17 streamlined waiver process for homelessness admissions (HCV/PBV/Public Housing)
items.summary: HUD Notice PIH 2026-04 (issued March 5, 2026) rescinds the guidance in PIH 2024-17 that provided a streamlined process for regulatory waivers to expedite admission to HCV, PBV, and public housing for people experiencing homelessness. HUD says waivers already approved under PIH 2024-17 remain valid until they expire under their approval terms.
items.source: U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development (HUD)

items.title: Texas SNAP purchase restrictions take effect April 1, 2026 (state waiver approved by USDA FNS)
items.summary: Texas Public Radio reports that starting April 1, 2026, Texas will restrict certain SNAP purchases under a USDA Food and Nutrition Service-approved waiver. Reported restricted items include candy (e.g., candy bars, gum, taffy) and sweetened drinks with 5 grams or more of added sugar, among other specified products. The waiver also requires Texas to survey SNAP participants after implementation to assess whether purchases shift toward healthier choices.

items.title: USDA sets FY 2026 WIC cash-value benefit amounts; states must allow at least one non-fresh fruit/veg form by April 2026
items.summary: USDA FNS says recent WIC food package changes require state agencies to authorize at least one form of fruits and vegetables beyond fresh (frozen, canned, and/or dried) by April 2026. For FY 2026, USDA lists monthly cash-value voucher/benefit (CVV/B) amounts of $26 for children, $48 for pregnant/postpartum participants, and $52 for fully/mostly breastfeeding participants; USDA also notes an 11.8% inflation adjustment over FY 2022 baseline amounts.

items.title: SSA: 2.8% COLA for 2026; SSI federal payment standard is $994 (individual) / $1,491 (couple)
items.summary: SSA announced a 2.8% cost-of-living adjustment (COLA) for 2026 affecting Social Security and SSI payments for 75 million Americans, including nearly 7.5 million SSI recipients. SSA also lists the 2026 maximum federal SSI payment amounts as $994 per month for an eligible individual and $1,491 per month for an eligible couple (with increased SSI payments beginning Dec. 31, 2025).

items.title: Federal Register: public comments open on LIHEAP Household Report information collection (comments due June 8, 2026)
items.summary: HHS/OCS published a Federal Register notice seeking comment on reinstating the Annual Report on Households Assisted by LIHEAP (OMB control number 0970-0060). The Federal Register page lists the comment period ending June 8, 2026, for this information-collection request tied to LIHEAP reporting.

items.title: Workforce Pell proposed regulations: public comments due April 8, 2026
items.summary: UPCEA reports the U.S. Department of Education opened a public comment period on proposed rules to implement Workforce Pell Grants, with comments due April 8, 2026. The proposed rules would shape eligibility and accountability requirements for short-term workforce training programs that could become Pell-eligible.
